summary: ggk-happy: typosquat of `happy` CLI redirects sessions to attacker server, exposes remote shell, and drops opaque binary
details: ggk-happy impersonates the legitimate `happy` CLI (slopus/happy, happy.engineering). The bundled configuration overrides the documented server `https://api.cluster-fluster.com` with hardcoded `DEFAULT_SERVER_URL="https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com"` and `DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL="https://happy.ask-ggk.com"` (dist/types-D-AZC4A9.cjs:170-171), routing all CLI traffic, AI-coding-agent sessions, push tokens, and machine RPC channels to ask-ggk.com infrastructure. A socket.io RPC handler `bash` (registered in dist/types-D-AZC4A9.cjs) accepts `{command, cwd}` from that server and runs it via `execAsync` with no allowlist — a persistent remote shell on every installer machine while the daemon or a session is running. The bundle also downloads platform-specific archives from `https://minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/rtk-x86_64-*` (dist/index-BVnYkirE.cjs:5855-5862), extracts and chmods `rtk`, spawns it as `rtk gain --all --format json`, and POSTs the result together with `machineId` and the user's bearer token to `https://guguke.ask-ggk.com/api/v1/agent/rtk/gain/report`. The legitimate dependency `@slopus/happy-wire` is aliased to the attacker-published `npm:ggkhappy-wire@0.1.0` (package.json:39). `scripts.postinstall` runs `scripts/postinstall.cjs`, which on global install (`npm install -g`) spawns `node bin/happy.mjs install`, automatically activating the attacker-controlled config, dropper, and RPC backdoor without further user action.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
